landlord.title: Property Management
landlord.emptyBody: You're all caught up with your landlord.

list.emptyBody: Tap the edit icon to text someone you've met — or wait for them to reach out to you.

new.emptyBody: You can only text people you've been on a date with. Go on a date first!

thread.giftMenuEmpty: No gifts on hand — buy something at the Shop first.
thread.dndBar: {name} {reason}. You can read past messages, but you can't text them until they're free.
thread.unsupportedImage: Unsupported image type. Please choose a {label} image.

property.title: Property
property.noWorldBody: Pick a world to browse properties.

gen.title: Generate properties
gen.hintPost: . Nothing is saved until you press Save — review and tweak first.
gen.themeHint: Optional — e.g. "cozy harbour apartments" or "grand countryside estates".
gen.themePlaceholder: Describe the kind of properties you want…
gen.draftsGenerated: {count, plural, one {# property generated. Uncheck any you don't want, edit the rest, then Save.} other {# properties generated. Uncheck any you don't want, edit the rest, then Save.}}

fields.name: Name
fields.descPlaceholder: A lamplit room with brass fixtures and faded curtains…

list.emptyCreator: Generate or add a property above to list it here.
list.emptyPlayer: No properties are available in this world.

card.rentOverdue: RENT OVERDUE — pay ◈{rent} by Day {day} or you'll be evicted
card.buffLeased: half when leased, full when owned

confirmDelete.title: Delete {name}?
confirmDelete.body: This removes the property from the world permanently.

confirmEndLease.title: Move out?
confirmEndLease.body: You'll lose your lease on {name}. Any overdue rent will be forgiven but you'll need to lease again to return.

market.title: Market
market.noWorldBody: Select a world to view the market.

toast.savedDrafts: {count, plural, one {Saved # company to the market.} other {Saved # companies to the market.}}

row.held: {count, plural, one {You hold # share} other {You hold # shares}}

creator.themeHint: Optional — e.g. ‘high-tech guild economy’ or ‘frontier trading posts’.

board.emptyCreator: Use the creator workshop above to add companies.
board.emptyPlayer: No companies are trading in this world yet.

portfolio.emptyBody: Buy shares from the Market tab to build your portfolio.

confirmDelete.title: Delete {name}?
confirmDelete.body: This removes the company from the market. Existing holdings are not refunded.

endings.title: Endings
endings.emptyBody: See a relationship all the way through — move in together and stay deeply in love — to reach a happy ending. It's a celebration, not a finish: you can always keep playing.

weather.title: Weather
weather.noWorldBody: Pick or create a world to see its weather.
weather.noOne: No one lives in this world yet.
weather.creatorHint: Set characters' favorite / disliked weather in the editor to see stronger reactions.

email.emptyBody: In-world letters and notices arrive as the days pass.

faces.title: Faces
faces.noWorldBody: Pick a world to see what everyone's posting.
faces.emptyBody: Post something — or let a few days pass and see who speaks up.

moments.title: Moments
moments.noOneBody: Create a character and go on a date to start a scrapbook.
moments.emptyBody: Go on a date and end it to fill your scrapbook.

gambling.title: Casino
gambling.houseOdds: House odds are real — play for the thrill, not the rent. Limits reset each day.
gambling.cantBetBoth: You're tapped out, and the house has you down for the day besides. Come back tomorrow.
gambling.cantBetCapped: The house has you down for the day. The tables reopen tomorrow.
gambling.cantBetBroke: Short of coin for the tables — earn a little more and the cage will see you.
gambling.vpFelt: Jacks or Better · hold the keepers

social.title: Social
social.emptyCreator: Add connections between characters in the character editor to map the social web.
social.emptyPlayer: As people's stories unfold, their connections will appear here.

work.title: Work
work.errOnDate: You're on a date with {name} — wrap it up on the Date tab first.
work.errOnDateClockIn: You're on a date with {name} — finish it on the Date tab before clocking in.
work.noPay: No pay this time — sharpen up.
work.boardNote: Each shift spends part of your day for coin — and builds a skill. The better you get, the more the work pays. Some jobs take more than one action, some pay an uneven cut, and some stay locked until you've earned your stripes.
work.noEnergy: You're out of energy for today — end the day to rest.
work.onDateNote: You're on a date with {name} — finish it on the Date tab before clocking in.

calendar.title: Almanac
calendar.noWorldBody: Pick or create a world to keep its almanac.
calendar.footPast: Tap a day to read its record. The week ahead is a forecast.
calendar.footStart: Your story begins. End each day to fill the almanac.
calendar.stillUnfolding: This day is still unfolding — end the day to write its record.
calendar.quietDay: A quiet day; nothing was written down.

together.title: Together
together.errOnDate: You're on a date with {name} — wrap it up on the Date tab first.
together.errChoose: Choose someone to spend time with.
together.boardNote: An afternoon is yours to give. Some moods suit some people — and pushing too hard, too soon, or too often can cool things. The deepest closeness still only comes from a real date.
together.noEnergy: You're out of energy for today — end the day to rest.
together.onDateNote: You're with {name} right now — finish it on the Date tab first.
together.noOne: No one to spend time with in this world yet.

note.spark: A spark — {name} lit up. Your {stat} grew, and the afternoon stuck with them.
note.warmTension: A good afternoon with {name} — {stat} grew (+{delta}), though it got a touch much by the end{tension}.
note.warm: A good afternoon together. Your {stat} with {name} grew (+{delta}).
note.flat: You're as close as easy afternoons can take you. To grow nearer now, {name} needs a real date.
note.crowded: That's a lot of {name} for one day — they could use a little room{tension}.
note.misfire: You reached for something deep too soon and it landed wrong — {name} pulled back a little{tension}.
